text: What if I told you that time can decay? Half-life is the time it takes for half the nuclei in a radioactive sample to decay. Imagine a clock that counts down, but instead of seconds, it counts down atoms! For example, Carbon-14 has a half-life of about 5,730 years. After this time, half of the original amount has decayed. This decay helps scientists date ancient artifacts and understand the age of our planet. Want to learn more about the mysteries of science? Hit follow for more fascinating facts!
